author: @michiganengineer8621
text: The multi-million dollar question would be.  Why do auto manufacturer's use the much more complex Torsen differential over the Archimedean?

author: @kevinolson1102
text: Re the Torsen differential - Interestingly, Maurice Walter's patent of 1915 (US1164770) is hardly functionally different from the Torsen, using worms with parallel axes.  These were quite famously used the Walter Snow Fighter plow trucks and airport crash trucks with "Four Point Positive Drive".  I would point out the Maurice was a candy maker (and candy machine maker) in New York City before he became a automobile and truck manufacturer in Upstate New York.

author: @richardglickman7203
text:
I have a few questions related to all your projects. Answering them may result in an interesting video.
How many printers are you actually using? Which ones? Why?
How many design programs do you use? Which ones? Why?
How much time do you spend, weekly, on developing a project? 
How much filament do you use weekly?
What am I missing related to producing a project?
I very much enjoy your channel and wonder what is involved in developing and producing a project.

author: @Gomorragh
text: something i saw the other day, a stripped down MRI machine, and it made me wonder if the method used to run the electromagnets on that could be used in reverse to create electricity, also part of the reason i thought of this is using these differentials to mount the generation point on the ground rather than directly on the rotor

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: in theory yeah it could and i like the idea of the ground based generator

replies.author: @Gomorragh
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering well its always struck me as sort of stupid the way theyre stripped down to the point they cant be cooled easily because they need to be light enough to be up on a giant metal stick. But it was the same with electric engines, for years i was talking to an electrician friend about why electric engines are not geared, (i know torque is part of it) but then after 15+ years of being told no its pointless, someone goes and does it. But its in addition, all the electricity that we lose thanks to the breaks being put onto wind turbines so they dont overload the national grid was thought up by lunatics, if they had been put offshore, they could have been routed through a system where, if there is extra power it would run desalination plants to refill the low water reservoirs, or some other thing that would in other countries be used to make a solid state storage system but the UK seems it cant get its thick head around

author: @briancooke4259
text: You are terrific!  Truly looking forward by looking backward, I really thought this would be related to the Archimedes drive, you seemed so excited about that.  Have a blessed day.

author: @FuhrChris
text:
Assuming that both were made from metal
 Which one would have less wear and tear over time?
In short:
If someone used a series of these in say a Wind Wall.  Or  Several small wind mills up on towers.
Which one would require less tinkering trying to keep the whole system up and running?

(I'm prioritizing "Maintance" over issues of drag simply because I don't like working in High places;  And had a friend who nearly lost their life to small farm sized windmill collapse during an attempt to change out the some worn parts in the generator.  The family is still trying to finically recover.) (I'm not asking which system has more drag due to things like the number of parts etc. or the potential for power loss via some method of transmission.)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: less parts usually means less maintenance mate

author: @Bill-n9l7d
text: I did enjoy the video. Differencals are very cool on a car they help keep wear and tear down on the rear wheel by reducing road friction at the rear drive wheels. The draw back is if you get in the mud one wheel spins while the other doesn't.  They did use to have positive traction rear ends so mud wasn't a big issue.  But here again the wear on the tires just isn't worth it. I know that on 18 wheel truck the differencals have air switches that change it from low speed to high speed to get extra gears. If I didn't get it exactly right someone jump right on in.
